message:
We all grow up believing a wagging tail means a happy dog. Mostly true — but the full story is a lot more interesting. 🐶

A dog's tail is one of their loudest communication tools, and the details matter. A high, stiff wag can signal alertness or confidence, while a low, loose sweep usually means a relaxed, friendly pup. A tucked tail often says "I'm nervous." Scientists have even found that dogs tend to wag a little more to the RIGHT when they're feeling positive, and more to the LEFT when something makes them uneasy — and other dogs can actually read the difference.

So next time your dog greets you, watch the whole picture — the height, the speed, the direction. Your dog is telling you exactly how they feel. 💛

What's your dog's signature happy-tail move? Tell me about it below! 🐾
